title: trifolium agrestinum subsp. littorale
intro: Trifolium agrestinum subsp. littorale, also known as red clover, is a perennial plant belonging to the legume family and is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, anticoagulant, and estrogenic effects. Red clover is known to have health benefits, such as reducing menopausal symptoms, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting bone health. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, capsules, and tinctures, making it a popular supplement in traditional medicine.
